_meta.description: Structured acceptance criteria for Chargeback Evidence Pack Builder v1. Do not delete entries. Update status field only. Add new entries at the end of each category array.

features.description: User can create an account with email and password
steps: Enter password meeting minimum requirements (8+ chars)
passes: Account created in Supabase Auth
Verification email sent
User redirected to dashboard after verification
No duplicate account can be created with same email
features.notes: Must use Supabase Auth. No OAuth in v1.

features.description: User can log in with email and password
passes: User is authenticated and redirected to dashboard
Invalid credentials show error message (not which field is wrong)
Session persists across browser refresh
Brute force protection active (rate limiting)
features.notes: Use Supabase session. Error message must not reveal whether email or password is wrong.

features.description: User can reset password via email
passes: Reset email sent if account exists (silent if not)
Reset link expires after 1 hour
New password accepted and user logged in
features.notes: Silent behavior on non-existent email prevents account enumeration.

features.description: User can create a new dispute pack with basic metadata
steps: Click New Dispute Pack from dashboard
passes: Pack record created in database with status Draft
Deadline visible on pack card in dashboard
All fields validated (amount is numeric, date is in the future)
Pack ID generated and stored
features.notes: Stripe dispute ID is optional but should be validated as a Stripe dispute ID format if provided (ch_xxx or dp_xxx).

features.description: User can select a dispute reason category
steps: After creating pack metadata, view category selection screen
Select one of: Fraudulent, Product Not Received, Product Not as Described, Subscription Cancelled, Duplicate Charge, Credit Not Processed
passes: Category stored on pack record
Category-specific intake form displayed immediately after selection
Category-specific evidence checklist previewed in sidebar or below form
User cannot proceed without selecting a category
features.notes: This is MAGIC MOMENT #1: category selection must immediately reveal the specific evidence checklist. This is the aha moment.

features.description: User answers category-specific intake questionnaire
steps: View intake questions specific to selected dispute category
passes: All required fields validated before proceeding
Answers stored to database
Partial answers auto-saved on blur/change
User can go back and edit answers before generation
No open-ended text prompts — all fields are structured (dropdowns, checkboxes, date pickers, short inputs)
features.notes: MAGIC MOMENT #2: after answering 2-3 questions, show a partial narrative preview forming in real time. This demonstrates value before payment.

features.description: User can upload and label exhibits
steps: Upload file (PDF, PNG, JPG accepted; max 10MB per file; max 20 files)
Enter label for each exhibit (e.g., Exhibit A: Order Confirmation)
passes: Files uploaded to Supabase Storage
File type and size validated before upload
Labels required before proceeding to generation
File list persists on page refresh
Rejected file types show clear error
features.notes: Storage uses signed URLs. Files never publicly accessible. Max 20 exhibits enforced server-side.

features.description: Evidence gap detection warns user of missing critical exhibits
steps: System flags any critical evidence items that are missing based on category
passes: Missing critical items shown with clear warning (not blocking — user can proceed)
Warning message explains why each item matters
User can acknowledge and proceed or go back to upload
features.notes: Gap detection is a key anti-commodity feature. Must be category-specific. Non-blocking by design — merchant may legitimately lack some evidence.

features.description: User is gated at pack generation with a payment requirement
steps: If no pack credits available, redirected to payment selection
passes: Stripe Checkout session created server-side
User redirected to Stripe-hosted Checkout
No payment info stored in app database
Stripe publishable key used client-side only
features.notes: Payment gate must be server-side enforced. Client-side credit checks are for UX only. Server re-validates credits on generation request.

features.description: Stripe webhook provisions pack credit after successful payment
steps: Server verifies webhook signature with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Server provisions 1 or 3 pack credits to user account
User redirected to dashboard with credit available
passes: Webhook signature verified using raw body (not parsed JSON)
Idempotency key prevents duplicate credit grant on webhook retry
Credit visible in dashboard immediately after redirect
Failed webhook delivery logged and alertable
features.notes: CRITICAL: Raw body required for Stripe signature verification. Never use parsed req.body. Use stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, secret).

features.description: Pack credit is consumed on PDF generation
steps: User clicks Generate Pack (with all required fields completed)
Server atomically checks credit, deducts 1, begins generation
passes: Credit deducted before generation begins (not after)
If generation fails, credit is restored
Credit count updated in UI after generation
Double-generation prevented (idempotency on pack generation request)
features.notes: Atomic deduction prevents race condition where user triggers two simultaneous generations.

features.description: AI generates category-specific rebuttal narrative from intake answers
steps: System calls OpenAI GPT-4o with category-specific prompt and sanitized merchant answers
passes: Narrative is professional, third-person dispute language
Narrative does not contain guaranteed win language
Narrative references specific intake answers (not generic boilerplate)
Narrative does not exceed 600 words
Generation completes within 30 seconds
features.notes: API key never exposed client-side. Prompt is category-specific, not open-ended. Output must be reviewed in QA for each category before launch.

features.description: PDF assembled with correct structure and labeling
steps: After narrative generation, system assembles PDF
PDF structure: Cover page → Category checklist (checked/unchecked) → Rebuttal narrative → Labeled exhibits in order
passes: Cover page shows: merchant name, dispute amount, dispute category, response deadline, generation date
Checklist shows all category evidence items with checkmark if uploaded, empty if missing
Narrative section clearly labeled
Each exhibit labeled with merchant-provided label (Exhibit A, B, etc.)
PDF passes basic readability check (no truncated text, no overlapping elements)
PDF file size under 20MB
features.notes: MAGIC MOMENT #3: The first open of this PDF must look like a lawyer produced it. Visual quality is non-negotiable.

features.description: Generated PDF available for download for 72 hours
steps: After generation, PDF stored in Supabase Storage
expires_at set to now + 72 hours on pack record
User sees countdown timer on pack card
passes: Download returns signed URL valid for download session only
Expired packs show Expired status with option to regenerate
Expired files deleted from storage by background job
User warned at generation time that pack expires in 72 hours
features.notes: Retention policy: 72h for files, 90d for intake answers. Both purged on account deletion.

features.description: Each pack shows response deadline with visual urgency
steps: See list of dispute packs with deadlines
passes: Deadline shown on every pack card
Packs with deadline within 7 days shown with yellow warning indicator
Packs with deadline within 48 hours shown with red urgent indicator
Expired deadlines shown with grey Missed indicator
Packs sorted by deadline proximity by default
features.notes: No email reminders in v1. Visual indicators only. Email reminders are v2.

features.description: User can manually mark a pack as Submitted
passes: Pack status updated to Submitted
Submitted date stored
Pack no longer shows deadline urgency indicators
Status visible on dashboard
features.notes: No automatic submission integration in v1. Manual only.

features.description: No guaranteed win language appears anywhere in the product
steps: Review all UI copy, error messages, email templates, PDF output
Search for: win, guarantee, guaranteed, promise, certain, definitely, assured
passes: Zero instances of guaranteed win language found
All outcome-related language uses: strongest possible, best shot, submission-ready, complete evidence
PDF cover page includes disclaimer: This pack is prepared to support your dispute response. Card network decisions are final and cannot be guaranteed.
features.notes: This is a legal and trust requirement. Failure here is a launch blocker.

features.description: Retention policy disclosed clearly to users
passes: Generation screen shows: Your pack will be available to download for 72 hours. After that, your uploaded files are permanently deleted.
Account settings shows intake answer retention: Your answers are retained for 90 days to allow re-generation.
Privacy policy accurately describes retention policy
features.notes: Users uploading sensitive business documents need explicit data handling disclosure.

features.description: Stripe webhook verified with signature before any action
passes: Invalid signatures return 400 and take no action
Valid signatures proceed to provisioning
Webhook handler does not use parsed JSON body for signature verification
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET set via environment variable, never hardcoded
features.notes: CRITICAL security requirement. Failure here means payments can be spoofed.

features.description: Category selection immediately reveals specific evidence checklist (Magic Moment #1)
steps: Evidence checklist for that category appears without page reload
Checklist shows specific document names, not generic items
passes: Checklist visible within 300ms of category selection
Checklist items are specific to category (e.g., AVS/CVV match for Fraudulent, not generic)
User reaction: Oh, it knows exactly what I need
Checklist updates if user changes category selection
features.notes: This is the primary aha moment. If this feels slow or generic, the product feels like a template. Must feel instant and specific.

features.description: Partial narrative preview forms as user answers intake questions (Magic Moment #2)
steps: Partial narrative snippet appears in sidebar or below form
Snippet updates as user continues answering
passes: Preview snippet appears after question 2 is answered
Snippet uses professional dispute language with merchant's actual answers
Snippet is clearly labeled: Preview of your rebuttal narrative
Full narrative not shown (preserves generation incentive)
features.notes: This demonstrates value before payment. Critical for conversion. Can be pre-generated client-side from a template for speed — does not need to call AI.

features.description: Downloaded PDF looks professionally produced (Magic Moment #3)
steps: Opens PDF in any standard PDF viewer
passes: Cover page has clear professional layout with all key details
Section headers are clearly formatted and labeled
Exhibits are labeled with merchant-provided labels
Font is readable (min 11pt body text)
Page numbers present
No lorem ipsum, placeholder text, or template artifacts
Reviewer impression: This looks like a lawyer produced it
features.notes: This is the moment that justifies $39 and drives word-of-mouth. Visual quality is non-negotiable. QA must review actual PDF output before launch.
